mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in existing home sales and housing starts indicates a collapsing housing sector that drags down broader economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 60% in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a month-over-month rebound in the index signals a supply-side recovery that triggers short-term equity market rallies over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds during contraction phases yield negative 5-day forward returns in >70% of cases, invalidating the recovery rally mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in >65% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 60-day returns, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, the combination of weakening demand and restrictive financing conditions produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of slowing GDPC1 and high FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns, with positive return frequency exceeding 55% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in existing home sales signals renewed consumer confidence and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases during recovery phases yield negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive significance when housing inventory data is unavailable.
